title: antirrhinum majus subsp. tortuosum
intro: Antirrhinum majus subsp. tortuosum is a subspecies of the common snapdragon plant, known for its unique, twisted or bent flower spikes and fragrant, tubular flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Antirrhinum majus subsp. tortuosum have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory problems. Antirrhinum majus subsp. tortuosum can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy for various health concerns.
